name: cobaton-demo
description: 并笔 CoBaton — a conflict-resolution / write-arbitration layer that sits UNDER any editor for human+agent co-editing of the same live document. Instead of merely avoiding collisions (isolate-by-section + flicker-free rendering, as OpenMarkdown does), CoBaton RESOLVES them: a deterministic region-ownership state machine + a baton hand-off protocol (human presence claims a region; the agent announces intended regions; overlaps are DEFERRED, not dropped; deferred agent edits are RE-VALIDATED against the human's latest changes before landing). It guarantees: never touch the region you're actively editing, never silently drop your edits, never waste the agent's work on a region you're about to change, always re-validate a deferred edit. A real deterministic engine replays the SAME co-editing event trace through both a naive (last-write-wins) mode and CoBaton mode, so the before/after value (lost edits / interruptions / wasted agent edits) is measured, not faked. Static frontend demo (mock doc + scripted timeline, no backend/LLM/real agent/external API).
